fields.name: turn_detection
fields.description: Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

fields.name: input_audio_noise_reduction
fields.description:
Configuration for input audio noise reduction. This can be set to `null` to turn off.
Noise reduction filters audio added to the input audio buffer before it is sent to VAD and the model.
Filtering the audio can improve VAD and turn detection accuracy (reducing false positives) and model performance by improving perception of the input audio.

fields.name: input_audio_format
fields.description:
The format of input audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.
For `pcm16`, input audio must be 16-bit PCM at a 24kHz sample rate,
single channel (mono), and little-endian byte order.

fields.name: include
fields.description:
The set of items to include in the transcription. Current available items are:
`item.input_audio_transcription.logprobs`

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateRequestTurnDetection

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection. Only `server_vad` is currently supported for transcription sessions.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description:
Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults
to 500ms. With shorter values the model will respond more quickly,
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

types.description: Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateRequestInputAudioNoiseReduction
types.description:
Configuration for input audio noise reduction. This can be set to `null` to turn off.
Noise reduction filters audio added to the input audio buffer before it is sent to VAD and the model.
Filtering the audio can improve VAD and turn detection accuracy (reducing false positives) and model performance by improving perception of the input audio.

types.name: NoiseReductionType
types.description: Type of noise reduction. `near_field` is for close-talking microphones such as headphones, `far_field` is for far-field microphones such as laptop or conference room microphones.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateRequestInputAudioFormat
types.description:
The format of input audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.
For `pcm16`, input audio must be 16-bit PCM at a 24kHz sample rate,
single channel (mono), and little-endian byte order.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model to use for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`. Use `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` when you need diarization with speaker labels.

fields.name: language
fields.description:
The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in
[ISO-639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) (e.g. `en`) format
will improve accuracy and latency.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description:
An optional text to guide the model's style or continue a previous audio
segment.
For `whisper-1`, the [prompt is a list of keywords](/docs/guides/speech-to-text#prompting).
For `gpt-4o-transcribe` models (excluding `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`), the prompt is a free text string, for example "expect words related to technology".
Prompt is not supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

fields.name: delay
fields.description:
Controls how long the model waits before emitting transcription text.
Higher values can improve transcription accuracy at the cost of latency.
Only supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

types.name: AudioTranscriptionModel
types.description: The model to use for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`. Use `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` when you need diarization with speaker labels.

types.name: AudioTranscriptionDelay
types.description:
Controls how long the model waits before emitting transcription text.
Higher values can improve transcription accuracy at the cost of latency.
Only supported with `gpt-realtime-whisper` in GA Realtime sessions.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateResponse

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description:
Ephemeral key returned by the API. Only present when the session is
created on the server via REST API.

fields.name: modalities
fields.description:
The set of modalities the model can respond with. To disable audio,
set this to ["text"].

fields.name: input_audio_format
fields.description: The format of input audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.

fields.name: turn_detection
fields.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

types.description:
A new Realtime transcription session configuration.

When a session is created on the server via REST API, the session object
also contains an ephemeral key. Default TTL for keys is 10 minutes. This
property is not present when a session is updated via the WebSocket API.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateResponseClientSecret

fields.name: value
fields.description:
Ephemeral key usable in client environments to authenticate connections
to the Realtime API. Use this in client-side environments rather than
a standard API token, which should only be used server-side.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description:
Timestamp for when the token expires. Currently, all tokens expire
after one minute.

types.description:
Ephemeral key returned by the API. Only present when the session is
created on the server via REST API.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model used for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`.

fields.name: language
fields.description: The language of the input audio.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description: The prompt configured for input audio transcription, when present.

types.name: RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateResponseTurnDetection

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection, only `server_vad` is currently supported.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description:
Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults
to 500ms. With shorter values the model will respond more quickly,
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

types.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

methods.description:
Create an ephemeral API token for use in client-side applications with the
Realtime API specifically for realtime transcriptions. 
Can be configured with the same session parameters as the `transcription_session.update` client event.

It responds with a session object, plus a `client_secret` key which contains
a usable ephemeral API token that can be used to authenticate browser clients
for the Realtime API.

Returns the created Realtime transcription session object, plus an ephemeral key.

requestBody.description: Create an ephemeral API key with the given session configuration.
